# cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_instances (Data Source)

Used to retrieve all Public Cloud VM instances of the tenant, optionally filtered. The full result set is paginated automatically.

To query this datasource you will need the `public_cloud_vm_instances_read` role.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# List all running VM instances of the tenant (the result set is paginated
# automatically). Filters are optional.
data "cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_instances" "running" {
  status = "running"
}

output "running_vm_names" {
  value = [for vm in data.cloudtemple_public_cloud_vm_instances.running.instances : vm.name]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `availability_zone_id` (String) Filter by availability zone ID.
- `instance_family_id` (String) Filter by instance family ID.
- `name` (String) Filter by exact VM name.
- `order_by` (String) Field to order by (`name`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `status`, `vcpu`, `ramGb`).
- `order_dir` (String) Order direction (`asc` or `desc`).
- `status` (String) Filter by status (e.g. `running`, `stopped`).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `instances` (List of Object) The list of VM instances. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instances))

- `disks_size_gb` (Number)
- `guest_tools_installed` (Boolean)
- `id` (String)
- `instance_family` (List of Object) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedobjatt--instances--instance_family))
